title: myrsine youngii
intro: Myrsine youngii, also known as Ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant species native to Hawaii, belonging to the family Myrsinaceae. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing protection against infections and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Myrsine youngii is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
